title: Boater remains missing after crash off of Salisbury Beach
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A boat crashed into the jetty at Salisbury Beach Friday night, leaving one man missing.
The missing boater, identified by relatives as 49-year-old Vilmar Dorner, was not found despite extensive search efforts.
The U.S. Coast Guard suspended its search on Saturday evening after combing the area with no results.

title: After officiating Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding, Adam Sandler played pickup basketball in this Mass. vacation hotspot
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Adam Sandler officiated Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding at Madison Square Garden on Friday evening.
Sandler played pickup basketball on Nantucket two days later after calling local police for game locations.
Nantucket players praised Sandler's skills, noting his elite passing, and took photos with him after the game.

title: Local obituary: Oliver Lewis, 15, ‘an old soul, with wisdom far beyond his years’
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Oliver Lewis, 15, of Franklin, Massachusetts, died from injuries in a bicycle accident on June 23, 2026.
Oliver was known for his kindness, intelligence, and faith, excelling in academics and sports at Bishop Feehan High School.
His organ donations saved five lives, and a scholarship fund was established in his name at Bishop Feehan High School.

title: Poll: Most in Mass. think police hid evidence in Karen Read case
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: 69% of Massachusetts residents polled believe police probably concealed evidence in the Karen Read case, per a UNH poll.
Nearly two-thirds think police conduct in Read's case signals broader policing issues in Massachusetts, with varying beliefs across political lines.
86% of respondents support the right to sue police departments for rights violations, but opinions on police performance vary significantly.

title: Duxbury man drowns while swimming to his boat
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: James Merlin, 66, drowned in Duxbury Bay on Sunday while swimming to his boat, authorities reported.
Merlin was rescued by bystanders, received CPR from an off-duty officer, and was taken to Beth Israel Deaconess Hospital.
State police are investigating, but Plymouth County DA confirmed no foul play is suspected in Merlin's death.

title: Groveland ice cream shop goes viral with scoop-tossing video
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A video of Groveland's Jeff and Maria's Ice Cream shop garnered 23 million views on Instagram.
Owner Jeff Boragine continues to operate the ice cream shop in memory of his late wife, Maria.
The shop's viral social media presence attracts visitors from afar, boosting its popularity and local recognition.

title: Celtics sign first-round pick Chris Cenac Jr.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics sign first-round pick Chris Cenac Jr., drafted No. 27 in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Cenac, a 6'11" forward, was a consensus top-ten recruit and former McDonald's All-American.
Celtics view Cenac as a developmental project, filling a need for athleticism in the front-court.

title: Local obituary: Riley J. Sergi, Central Catholic graduate from Dracut
author: Boston.com
bullets: Riley J. Sergi, 28, of Haverhill, died from injuries sustained in a car accident on June 28, 2026.
Sergi was a Dracut native, a Central Catholic High School graduate, and was studying to become an electrician.
He is survived by his parents Rhonda Lacy and David Sergi, as well as other family members in Massachusetts and beyond.

title: A seal pup was riding a boogie board off the coast of Maine, because of course it was
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: A seal pup rode a boogie board off Old Orchard Beach, Maine, over the Fourth of July weekend.
The seal pup reportedly took the boogie board from a tourist and returned to its colony on Stratton Island.
Sightings of seals on surfboards or boogie boards are rare but have been documented in places like San Diego.

title: ‘Huzzah!’: Boston’s sailing community is ready to welcome the Tall Ships
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 250 kicks off on July 11 with the Grand Parade of Sail, featuring nearly 50 Tall Ships.
Local yacht clubs are organizing flotillas to view the Tall Ships, celebrating Boston's maritime history.
The event includes educational opportunities for youth with involvement from non-profit sailing centers like Courageous Sailing.
